title: What Are AI Agents, Really?
subtitle: ChatGPT is a brain in a jar. An agent has a brain AND a body. The difference changes everything.

sections.heading: The Brain in a Jar
sections.content: A chatbot is an AI with no hands. It can think but not do. It answers questions but can't take action.

sections.heading: Now Give It a Body
sections.content: An agent can read files, write code, browse the web, call APIs, manage tasks. It goes from thinking to doing.

sections.heading: The 4 Layers of AI
sections.content: Layer 1: Chatbot (text in, text out). Layer 2: Assistant (tools). Layer 3: Agent (autonomous). Layer 4: Multi-agent (teams).

sections.heading: What Can Agents Actually Do Today?
sections.content: Code, research, manage projects, write content, analyze data, automate workflows. Not everything — but more than you think.

sections.heading: Why This Matters for You
sections.content: Agents are the next platform shift. Like internet → smartphone → AI agent. Understanding them now gives you a head start.

key_takeaways: A chatbot thinks; an agent thinks AND acts — it can read files, write code, browse, and call APIs
There are 4 layers of AI: chatbot, assistant, agent, and multi-agent teams
Agents are the next platform shift — understanding them now gives you a head start
